items.headline: Nepal cuts working week to five days as fuel shortage forces austerity
items.body: Nepal made Saturday a second weekly holiday for government offices and schools to conserve fuel amid shortages from disrupted Middle East oil supplies. The Himalayan nation relies almost entirely on India for fuel, leaving it highly exposed to global price shocks.

items.headline: China brokers resumed Pakistan-Afghanistan talks after weeks of deadly border fighting
items.body: Representatives from Pakistan and Afghanistan's Taliban government met in China's Urumqi after clashes killed hundreds since February. Ceasefire talks follow earlier rounds in Qatar and Turkey that collapsed after Pakistani strikes in Kabul.

items.headline: Burkina Faso forces killed twice as many civilians as jihadists since 2023, report finds
items.body: Government troops and allied militias killed over 1,200 civilians between January 2023 and August 2025, more than double those killed by militant groups, Human Rights Watch documented. The killings amount to war crimes and ethnic cleansing of Fulani communities.

uk.headline: Resident doctors begin six-day strike across England after rejecting government pay offer
uk.body: Junior doctors walked out over a pay dispute after the union rejected a deal including a 3.5% rise. NHS typically delivers 95% of normal activity during strikes, but routine appointments face disruption during the Easter period.

uk.headline: Two-child benefit cap abolished as Universal Credit payments expand to all children
uk.body: Scrapping the cap lifts approximately 450,000 children out of poverty by end of parliament. Families gain around 3,650 pounds per year for each third or subsequent child.

nl.headline: Netherlands commits €3.9 billion to new offshore wind farm in North Sea
nl.body: The government reserved funds for IJmuiden Ver Gamma-B, a gigawatt-scale project scheduled for 2032. Budget constraints forced scaling back from an earlier plan to tender two gigawatt sites this year.

nl.headline: Ter Apel asylum center drops below legal capacity for first time in months
nl.body: Fewer than 2,000 people stayed overnight, meeting the court-imposed limit after years of overcrowding. Emergency shelters in multiple municipalities absorbed transfers as a temporary winter facility closed.
